engaged.definition: Involved and paying attention; actively participating.
engaged.example: An engaged parent watches and listens carefully.

observation.definition: The act of watching something carefully.
observation.example: Careful observation helps you understand your child.

opportunity.example: She waited for an opportunity to speak.

compose.definition: To put together; to prepare something carefully.
compose.example: Instead of composing a reply, just listen.

response.definition: An answer or reaction to something.
response.example: Her response was kind and thoughtful.

reply.definition: An answer given back to someone.
reply.example: He thought about his reply before speaking.

scary.example: Letting someone affect you can feel scary.

unspoken.definition: Not said out loud; understood without words.
unspoken.example: There was an unspoken fear between them.

disappear.definition: To stop existing or being seen.
disappear.example: If you listen to others, you won't disappear.

drained.definition: Feeling very tired and without energy.
drained.example: She felt drained after caring for the baby all day.

neediness.definition: The state of needing a lot of attention or care.
neediness.example: A baby's neediness can feel overwhelming.

struggle.definition: A great effort to do something difficult.
struggle.example: It was a struggle to stay patient.

demands.definition: Strong requests that are hard to refuse.

defend.example: She tried to defend herself against feeling overwhelmed.

knack.definition: A natural skill or ability to do something well.
knack.example: She got the knack of reading the baby's signals.

pre-empting.definition: Doing something before it happens to prevent a problem.
pre-empting.example: She learned to pre-empt the baby's crying.

distressed.example: The baby became distressed when left alone.

commentary.definition: A description of what is happening as it happens.
commentary.example: She gave a running commentary as she did housework.

housework.definition: Work done to keep a house clean and tidy.
housework.example: She talked to the baby while doing housework.

tasks.definition: Jobs or pieces of work that need to be done.
tasks.example: Daily tasks can be shared with your child.

chat.definition: To talk in a friendly, informal way.
chat.example: The baby would chat back with sounds.

thrill.example: It's not that she got a thrill from looking at leaves.

examining.example: She found peace in examining small things with her child.

leaf.definition: The flat green part of a plant or tree.
leaf.example: The baby loved looking at every leaf.

ladybird.definition: A small round insect that is usually red with black spots.
ladybird.example: They watched a ladybird crawl across a leaf.

concentrate.definition: To focus your attention on something.
concentrate.example: She watched her baby concentrate on a toy.

awe.definition: A feeling of great respect and wonder.
awe.example: Watching her child filled her with awe.

responsive.example: The child was less responsive when talked at.

mellowed.definition: Became more gentle and relaxed over time.

expanded.example: She felt expanded by her child's influence.

incapable.example: She felt incapable of deep love before becoming a parent.

impart.definition: To share knowledge or information with someone.
impart.example: She learned to listen instead of just imparting her ideas.

pattern.definition: A regular way of doing or behaving.
pattern.example: She learned a new pattern of listening.

formed.example: They formed a deep and loving relationship.
